events.title: Bancroft Roundtable - Translating Saints: Nahua-Catholic Knowledges in the Bancroft Nahuatl Manuscript Collection
events.description: A roundtable discussion exploring Nahua-Catholic knowledges within the Bancroft Nahuatl Manuscript Collection.

events.title: California Golden Bears vs. Stanford Cardinal (Men's Basketball)
events.description: ACC men's basketball action featuring the California Golden Bears hosting the Stanford Cardinal.

events.title: Somi
events.description: Vocalist and composer Somi, a jazz artist, performs, blending Afropop, soul, and jazz.

events.title: Concrete Poetry with Lindsay Choi
events.description: A generative poetry workshop led by poet Lindsay Choi, taking inspiration from Theresa Hak Kyung Cha's concrete poetry.

events.title: Film Screening – “All the Walls Came Down”
events.description: A film screening chronicling the aftermath of the Eaton Fire, followed by a discussion with filmmaker Ondi Timoner and UC Berkeley Professor Jason Spingarn-Koff.

events.title: Canto Ostinato
events.description: An AMOC* & Sandbox Percussion Co-Production presenting a rarely performed opus by Dutch composer Simeon ten Holt.

events.title: A.I.M by Kyle Abraham
events.description: A performance by A.I.M by Kyle Abraham, part of Cal Performances' diverse program.

events.title: Takács Quartet
events.description: The season's second performance by the Takács Quartet, featuring a Bay Area premiere of a new work.

events.title: Kaffeeklatsch
events.description: A weekly German conversation group for students and campus community (runs to May 4).

events.title: Financial democracy or democratic finance?
events.description: Sociologist Mike McCarthy explores how democracy and working-class power can be strengthened by new deliberative forms of financial governance.

events.title: Remodeling Rationality: A History of Unorthodox Computing and AI
events.description: A lecture discussing the history of unorthodox computing and artificial intelligence.

events.title: Rebecca Solnit: Keynote | Public Land, Public Space, Public Discourse
events.location: 112 Bauer Wurster Hall, College of Environmental Design
events.description: A keynote address by Rebecca Solnit on the themes of public land, space, and discourse.

events.title: Bate-Papo
events.description: A weekly Portuguese conversation group for interested individuals (runs to May 5).

events.title: Clark Kerr Lecture #1: The American University in Crisis - Evolving Conditions
events.description: The first Clark Kerr Lecture focusing on the evolving conditions and crisis of the American University.

events.title: Responsibly Improving AI with Privacy-Sensitive Data: Principles, Theory, and Practice
events.description: A talk on the principles, theory, and practice of improving AI using privacy-sensitive data.

events.title: Impending Freedom: Palestine after the Human (Layal Ftouni)
events.description: A lecture by Layal Ftouni exploring the concept of Palestine after the human.

events.title: Poetry for the People: The June Jordan Experience
events.location: The Freight, 2020 Addison St, Berkeley, CA 94704
events.description: An event celebrating the 'Poetry for the People' movement inspired by June Jordan.

events.title: Métodos surgidos de la comunidad: colaboración a través de lugar y espacio | Un conversatorio con las defensoras del agua en Ecuador
events.description: A conversation with Ecuadorian water defenders on community-emergent methods and collaboration across place and space.

events.title: Berkeley Book Chat with Winnie Wong
events.description: A book chat session with author Winnie Wong.

events.title: Archaeology by and for Robots? Publishing, Data Curation, and Asserting Humanity in the AI Ecosystem
events.description: A discussion on the role of robots in archaeology, data curation, and humanity in the AI ecosystem.

events.title: Prof. Aron Rodrigue, “A Ladino Chronicle in the Ottoman Balkans: The Arie Family in the 19th century”
events.description: Professor Aron Rodrigue presents a chronicle of the Arie family in the Ottoman Balkans in the 19th century.

events.title: [Panel] Queer and Trans Perspectives on Labor Organizing
events.description: A panel discussion exploring queer and trans perspectives within labor organizing efforts.

events.title: Lecture: The Cultural Infrastructure of Oil in Iran and Iraq
events.description: A lecture on the cultural infrastructure surrounding oil in Iran and Iraq.

events.title: New frontiers in modelling nanoporous materials for catalysis, diffusion and adsorption by integrating machine learning in the molecular modelling workflow
events.description: Learn how integrating machine learning into molecular modeling is opening new frontiers in simulating nanoporous materials.

events.title: REI Colloquium: Bordering on Indifference: Immigration Agents Negotiating Race and Morality
events.description: A colloquium discussing immigration agents negotiating race and morality.

events.title: Finance, technology, and fascism today
events.description: Sociologist Fabian Muniesa explores the connection between contemporary conspiratorialist culture and Silicon Valley ideology.

events.title: Women on the Right in U.S. History: Intellectual, Economic, and Political Power
events.description: A conference extending the understanding of women's role in building conservative power in the United States.

events.title: 2026 Percy-Synar Awards Ceremony
events.location: Institute of Governmental Studies, 109 Philosophy Hall
events.description: An awards ceremony presented by the Institute of Governmental Studies.

events.title: Yaa Samar!
events.description: A performance presented by Cal Performances.

events.title: Chris Thile, mandolin and vocals
events.description: A musical performance by mandolinist and vocalist Chris Thile.

events.title: Annual Diller Lecture | “Philip Roth: Stung by Life” - A Conversation between Steven J. Zipperstein and Scott Saul
events.description: A conversation between Steven J. Zipperstein and Scott Saul on Philip Roth's work.

events.title: Myrl Beam: The Long Fire: Sexual Policing, Settler Colonialism, and the Minneapolis Uprising | Arcus/Places Prize Lecture
events.description: An Arcus/Places Prize Lecture by Myrl Beam discussing sexual policing, settler colonialism, and the Minneapolis Uprising.

events.title: 2026 Yin-Cheng Lecture: Dharma in Diaspora. Religiosity and Buddhist Practices in Italy's Chinese Communities
events.description: This lecture explores religious practices among the Chinese diaspora in Italy.

events.title: Workshop on Tannishō Commentarial Materials
events.location: Jodo Shinshu Center, 2140 Durant Avenue, Berkeley, CA
events.description: A workshop focused on commentarial materials related to Tannishō.

events.title: Evolution of Books and Preservation of Material Culture
events.description: An event exploring the evolution of books and the preservation of material culture.

events.title: Sonic Socialism: Governing Crisis through Sound in Urban Vietnam
events.description: A presentation on how sound is used to govern crisis in urban Vietnam.

events.title: Lecture: Spencer Lee-Lenfield and Kyung-Nyun Kim Richards on Theresa Hak-Kyung Cha
events.description: An illustrated presentation reconstructing Theresa Hak Kyung Cha's understanding of forces that shaped her life.

events.title: JACK Quartet
events.description: The JACK Quartet makes their Cal Performances debut with premieres of new works.

events.title: David Finckel, cello; Wu Han, piano
events.description: Cellist David Finckel and pianist Wu Han return to Berkeley to perform works by various composers.

events.title: 2026 California Neurotechnology Conference
events.description: A conference focusing on advancements and discussions in neurotechnology.

events.title: CGBC 2026: California Green Building Career Fair (Berkeley)
events.description: A career fair connecting employers in architecture, engineering, construction, sustainability, and renewable energy with students and professionals.
